meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Design Solutions for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to bypass design hurdles. A high-level guide for freelancers to ship professional apps faster using Antigravity and AI.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of design skills, leverage Claude Code to handle UI scaffolding while utilizing Antigravity to maintain structural integrity. By automating the visual component generation and enforcing design system constraints through AI, you can deliver pixel-perfect, high-converting interfaces without needing to be a professional UI designer.
content.intro:
You have the logic, the architecture, and the drive—but your UI looks like a relic from the early 2000s. In the freelance world, perception is reality; a clunky interface can kill a high-ticket project before the client even tests your backend. The bottleneck isn't your coding ability; it's the friction between a functional prototype and a polished production experience.

Enter the era of the AI-augmented developer. By integrating Claude Code with the Antigravity framework, you transform your terminal into a design-ops powerhouse. Stop struggling with CSS frameworks and start orchestrating AI agents to execute your design vision, allowing you to focus on the business logic that actually pays the bills.

steps.title: Environment Scaffolding
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-ready structure that prioritizes modular UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern, mobile-first project structure using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI components, ensuring strict separation of concerns between business logic and presentational layers.

steps.title: Design System Injection
steps.description: Feed your brand guidelines into the AI to ensure consistent visual output across all generated components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these brand guidelines and create a theme-config.json file that maps our color palette, typography, and spacing to standardized Tailwind classes.

steps.title: Component Generation
steps.description: Use AI to construct complex UI blocks based on your functional requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard sidebar using the theme-config.json. Include interactive states for active links and a collapsible mobile menu.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate sidebar component with theme-config constraints"

steps.title: Refinement and Accessibility
steps.description: Programmatically audit your components for accessibility and visual hierarchy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current component library for WCAG 2.1 compliance and suggest improvements to visual contrast and touch targets.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "audit current UI for accessibility and implement fixes"

steps.title: Final Integration
steps.description: Connect your AI-generated frontend to the backend API services.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unified API service hook that handles authentication state and displays loading/error states within the existing UI structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "integrate auth service with UI components"

pro_tips: Always define a 'design-language.txt' file in your root; Claude Code will reference this to avoid 'AI-generic' look and feel.
Use Antigravity to lock your component library versions so Claude Code doesn't hallucinate non-existent prop types.
Run 'claude diff' before every commit to catch layout shifts that the AI might have introduced during refactoring.
Inject your client's existing site URL into the prompt so the AI can clone their specific CSS variables for a seamless brand match.
Use modular prompt chains: generate the skeleton first, then the logic, then the styling, rather than asking for the whole page at once.
resources: Tailwind CSS Design System Guide: https://tailwindcss.com/docs
content.next_steps: Once your UI is scaffolding is complete, perform a final cross-browser sanity check and then move on to implementing your custom backend logic using the same agentic workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations dictate your project scope. With the right AI stack, you are no longer just a developer—you are a full-stack product studio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
